title: Today's AI Brief: Sesame, the conversational AI startup from Oculus founders, launches its iOS app
summary: A look at the latest AI news, including Sesame's iOS app launch and AI's impact on politics.
imageAlt: Image from source article for Sesame, the conversational AI startup from Oculus founders, launches its iOS app
imageCaption: Sesame, the conversational AI startup from Oculus founders, launches its iOS app
imageSourceTitle: Sesame, the conversational AI startup from Oculus founders, launches its iOS app

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's AI Brief. I've got a couple of interesting stories to share with you from the world of artificial intelligence. Grab a cup of coffee, get comfy, and let's dive in.

First up, Sesame, a conversational AI startup co-founded by Oculus' founders, has launched its iOS app. The company has been working on this for over a year, and it's now available in a public preview. Sesame's AI agents are designed to converse with users in a more natural way. I'm intrigued to see how this might change the way we interact with our devices. Imagine having a conversation with your phone that's more like chatting with a friend than typing commands.

Also making waves is the story of how AI tried to, well, bury a politician, but ended up making him more famous. It seems Anthropic and OpenAI spent a pretty penny trying to, ah, 'influence' the political landscape, but the real winner here is the politician himself. I find it amusing that AI's attempts to shape public opinion can sometimes backfire in hilarious ways. It's a great reminder that AI's impact on politics is still unpredictable and can lead to unexpected outcomes.

Sesame, the conversational AI startup from Oculus founders, launches its iOS app is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: On Thursday, the AI startup Sesame , co-founded by Oculus’ founders and others from the VR company that sold to Meta , released a public preview of the conversational AI agents it’s been developing for over a year. With its new iOS app , Sesame is rethinking the traditional AI chatbot experience popularized by apps like ChatGPT, creating one where conversat... The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

AI tried to bury this politician — now people have actually heard of him, from theverge.com, is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: Policy AI Anthropic AI tried to bury this politician — now people have actually heard of him When all is said and done, Anthropic and OpenAI will have spent millions in their political proxy war. But the real winner may be the guy they’re currently fighting over. When all is said and done, Anthropic and OpenAI will have spent millions in their political pro... The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
